content: p A former NASA administrator says he is  encouraged  that the US Congress is considering legislation to prevent NASA from spending more than 50 percent of its launch funding on any single provider.  p   p  America succeeds in space when American companies compete, innovate, and grow,  former NASA Administrator Jim Bridenstine  a href    on LinkedIn  a .  I m encouraged to see Congress taking meaningful steps to strengthen the industrial base that underpins both our civil and national security space missions.   p   p Bridenstine commended the chairman of the Senate Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation, Sen. Ted Cruz  R-Texas , and ranking member Sen. Maria Cantwell  D-Wash.  on a new provision that appears in the NASA Transition Authorization Act of 2025. Cruz plans to hold a  a href    hearing  a  for the legislation on Wednesday.  p  p  a href    full article  a   p   p  a href

metadata.author: Eric Berger
